key_insight: Sliding-window attention (SWA) becomes much more competitive for math reasoning when a pretrained self-attention model is first converted with supervised fine-tuning and then adapted with on-policy reinforcement learning.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical path to reduce long-context inference cost from quadratic to linear complexity without training a new base model from scratch.
method: Convert a pretrained self-attention model to SWA via supervised fine-tuning.
Apply reinforcement learning under the SWA constraint to adapt model trajectories.
Use the two-stage SWARR recipe to align training data and inference architecture.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports results on mathematical reasoning benchmarks, showing RL recovers much of the accuracy lost during SWA conversion while keeping linear-complexity attention benefits.
watch_item: SWA can still lag after SFT alone, and the method may depend on how well RL can reshape data trajectories to fit the sliding-window constraint.
paper_title: Architecture-Aware Reinforcement Learning Makes Sliding-Window Attention Competitive in Math Reasoning
